_comment: SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for the venue-card 'Area character' tags. Lives in knowledge/data/ (with taxonomy.md + tags.md) because it is STATIC taxonomy — trip-independent, not tied to any date, so it does not belong under trip-ni-july-2026/. update_report.py generates the tag colours (CSS), tooltips (TAGT), legend line, and emit order from this file; build_knowledge.py generates the tables in tags.md from it (via {{TAGTABLE:family}} placeholders). The only thing NOT here is how each pill's TEXT is computed from venue/route data — that stays in venue_tags(). Edit a tag's family/colour/tooltip/wording HERE and both the dashboard and the ? page follow. 'tags' array order = the fixed render order.

tags.tip: Typical share of wet days for your trip dates
tags.meaning: Share of wet days typical for your trip dates.

tags.tip: Rough travel time from the UK — from your sheet
tags.meaning: Rough door-to-crag travel time from the UK.

tags.tip: Minimum sensible trip length — from your sheet
tags.meaning: Shortest trip length worth the travel, weighed against your window.
Rock type — governs friction, drying and seepage.

tags.tip: Which way the crag faces — shifts felt temperature in sun, and windward faces catch the gusts
tags.meaning: Which way the crag faces (UKC: *Faces*) — north shades & cools (but stays wet longer), south bakes (but dries fast). A face looking into the forecast wind also takes the full gust penalty.

tags.tip: Sea-air crag — salt humidity and sea fog hold damp, so rock stays wet longer after rain
tags.meaning: Next to the sea — humid salt air and fog slow the rock's drying, so wet days cost this venue more.

tags.tip: Wind-exposed crag (sea cliff, free-standing tower, summit ridge) — gusts bite harder, no shelter
tags.meaning: No shelter from wind in any direction — the gust penalty is amplified here.

tags.tip: Curated drying-speed note — overrides the aspect+coastal derivation in the ranking
tags.meaning: How fast the rock sheds water after rain, curated per venue (e.g. Cornwall granite in a breeze vs slow Snowdonia rhyolite).

tags.tip: Estimated wall-height ceiling — from your sheet
tags.meaning: Estimated ceiling on wall height at the venue. *(was `height`)*

tags.tip: Approach character from route walk-in times
tags.meaning: Typical walk-in — median across indexed climbs.

tags.tip: Volume of multi-pitch climbing — from your sheet
tags.meaning: How much multi-pitch the area holds.

tags.tip: Trad grade range of nearby multi-pitch.com routes
tags.meaning: Trad grade range (British Adjectival System) across indexed climbs.

tags.tip: Longest route by pitch count on multi-pitch.com
Single tallest route nearby on multi-pitch.com
tags.meaning: The single tallest indexed climb, named. *(was `height`)*

tags.tip: Routes indexed on multi-pitch.com within 60 km
tags.meaning: How many climbs are indexed within 60 km — coverage & confidence.

tags.tip: Access/base is tide-dependent (sea cliff) — low-water times show on the weather tiles once the 10-day tide forecast reaches those dates
tags.meaning: Approach or base is tide-dependent — low-water times appear once your trip enters the 10-day tide forecast.

tags.tip: Route character / hazard flag from multi-pitch.com route data
tags.meaning: Union of every flagged climb nearby. Pill text mirrors the source flag.
tags.values: *Suffers Seepage* · *Loose Rock* · *Polished Rock* · *Abseil Required* · *Traverse Required* · *Boat approach* · *serious approach*
